_doc: Agent-authored judgment for 1589 Fulham St — the irreducible per-property inputs (vetted comps with listing-verified condition/quality grades, confirmed rates, narrative beats). build_packet.py derives the concluded value, grids, trend, and economics from this. Render: uv run python -m scripts.build_packet properties/fulham/judgment.json --output /tmp/review_fulham/packet.html

meta.banner: LIVE REVIEW — Ramsey assessment + sale data; structure from county cards (Beacon); condition + quality verified on listing photos. Not a filed appeal.

subject.style: 1923 stucco two-story — original; 1,440 SF above grade, unfinished basement, no garage

comps.descriptor: Renovated + addition (condition capped at −25%; upper bracket)
Original, large finished basement — only 690 SF above grade, set aside (context)

equalization.intro: Twelve size-matched neighborhood homes at their assessed values. The subject's building is assessed near the top of the range despite being original and lacking a finished basement or garage.
equalization.chart_caption: Assessed building $/SF vs. finished SF — subject (highlighted, $244) vs. size-matched neighborhood homes, with the neighborhood trend line. The subject sits ABOVE the trend; only renovated homes are higher.
equalization.land_observation: The subject's land is assessed in the lower third of its peers — fairly assessed. No land adjustment is sought; the entire issue is the building line.
equalization.building_observation: The subject's building is assessed at $244/SF of above-grade area — the top quartile of comparable homes (neighborhood median ≈ ${peer_median_bpsf}/SF), despite being original and lacking a finished basement or garage. Equalization corroborates the sales conclusion; the request rests on the sales evidence.
equalization.indicated_note: Building pulled to the neighborhood trend (${eq_psf}/SF at {subject_absf:,} SF, vs the assessed $244) = ${eq_psf} × {subject_absf:,} building + $182,600 land. Corroboration, not the requested value — the sales approach governs.

narrative.sales_subtitle: Primary method. Five arm's-length sales of similar-age homes within ~1 mile, each reduced to its above-grade value and adjusted to the subject as of January 2, 2026.
narrative.sales_intro: The subject has no finished basement and no garage, so each comp's basement and garage are removed before comparison. Structure is from the county's record cards; condition and construction quality were verified on listing photos.
narrative.subject_finding_title: An original home, assessed like an updated one with a finished basement and garage
narrative.subject_finding_body: 1589 Fulham is an original 1923 home — period kitchen and baths, original windows and woodwork — with 1,440 SF above grade, an unfinished basement, and no garage. The 2026 building assessment rose to $351,200 ($244/SF of above-grade area), the rate of homes that are updated and/or carry finished basements and garages this one does not have.
narrative.sales_finding_body: Recent arm's-length sales of similar-age homes nearby — reduced to their above-grade value and adjusted to the subject — bracket it and indicate a median of ${concluded:,} (mean ~${concluded_mean:,}), about {reduction_pct}% below the $533,800 assessment. The equalization analysis below shows the building assessed above the neighborhood, corroborating the same conclusion.
narrative.sales_reconciliation: The adjusted comps bracket the subject. The three size-matched, fully-adjustable homes indicate a median of ${concluded:,} (mean ~${concluded_mean:,}). The renovated 1369 Brompton, its condition capped at the credible −25% and carried at reduced weight, sets the upper bracket at ~${ceiling_value:,}, confirming the subject sits below renovated homes. We adopt the median, ${concluded:,}. (1829 Fulham is set aside — only 690 SF above grade, barely half the subject — shown in 4.1 for context.)
narrative.conclusion: 1589 Fulham is a well-kept but original 1923 home with 1,440 SF above grade, an unfinished basement, and no garage. For 2026 the county raised its building assessment to $351,200 ($244/SF of above-grade area) — the rate of updated homes that also carry finished basements and garages this one does not. Three size-matched sales, reduced to their above-grade value and adjusted to the subject, indicate a median of ${concluded:,} (mean ~${concluded_mean:,}); the renovated-plus-addition 1369 Brompton ($560,000), even with condition capped, ceilings near ${ceiling_value:,}, and 1829 Fulham is set aside (only 690 SF above grade). Equalizing the building line to the neighborhood trend independently indicates about ${eq_total:,}. We respectfully request the 2026 estimated market value be reduced from $533,800 to ${concluded:,} — a reduction of ${reduction:,} ({reduction_pct}%), entirely on the over-stated building line (~${annual_savings:,}/year in tax at the current rate).
